perturbation.original_text: A. In consideration for providing these Services, MKOS shall pay UTEK $120,000 in the form of unregistered shares of common stock (923,077 shares) upon the execution of this Strategic Alliance Agreement. 1/12th of the shares (76,923) shall vest each month during the term of this Agreement. In lieu of payment of shares, MKOS shall have the option of paying UTEK $10,000 per month for the Services described in this Agreement. If this Agreement is terminated any unvested shares will be returned to MKOS.
perturbation.changed_text: A. In consideration for providing these Services, MKOS shall pay UTEK compensation as agreed upon in writing. If this Agreement is terminated, any unvested shares will be returned to MKOS.
perturbation.explanation: The change omits the specific payment details ($120,000 in stock or $10,000 per month) and replaces it with a vague 'compensation as agreed upon in writing'. This introduces an in-text contradiction because Section IV previously defined the compensation clearly, now the current reference to 'compensation' lacks a concrete basis if there is no other agreement written. The reference to returning unvested shares creates inconsistency as the shares no longer have a clear baseline value since the payment method in Section IV is now unclear. If the agreement is terminated, and the payment form is ambigious, this will cause uncertainty in the contract because there is no longer any means to calculate what amount should be returned to MKOS. This omission results in a contradiction regarding how the compensation is supposed to be carried out.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. In consideration for providing these Services, MKOS shall pay UTEK compensation as agreed upon in writing. If this Agreement is terminated, any unvested shares will be returned to MKOS.
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the specific payment details ($120,000 in stock or $10,000 per month) and replaces it with a vague 'compensation as agreed upon in writing'. This introduces an in-text contradiction because Section IV previously defined the compensation clearly. The reference to returning unvested shares creates inconsistency as the shares no longer have a clear baseline value since the payment method in Section IV is now unclear. Because the payment form is ambiguous, this will cause uncertainty in the contract because there is no longer any means to calculate what amount should be returned to MKOS. This omission results in a contradiction regarding how the compensation is supposed to be carried out, as stated in the Perturbation Explanation. As the change alters the defintion of the compensation in the agreement it directly breaks compatibility with the contradicted section.

perturbation.original_text: F. On a case-by-case basis, at MKOS's request and UTEK's sole discretion, UTEK will propose an equity-financing plan for MKOS's consideration, to finance select technology acquisition opportunities for MKOS.
perturbation.changed_text: F. On a case-by-case basis, at MKOS's request, UTEK may consider financing options for MKOS.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'UTEK's sole discretion' and 'UTEK will propose an equity-financing plan', the change weakens UTEK's obligation to propose a plan. The original text gave UTEK discretion in proposing the plan, but they were still obligated to come up with one if requested. The modified version introduces ambiguity, as it's no longer clear that UTEK must formulate a specific plan. The modified version introduces ambiguity regarding the responsibility of UTEK for proposing an equity financing plan. The omission of UTEK's sole discretion creates uncertainty and could lead to disputes over whether UTEK is obligated to propose a plan at all. This contradiction exists because the original wording implied UTEK had to do something; the modified version turns it into something they only 'may consider', creating uncertainty in enforcement regarding their obligations
perturbation.contradicted_text: F. On a case-by-case basis, at MKOS's request, UTEK may consider financing options for MKOS.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that UTEK would propose an equity-financing plan at MKOS's request and UTEK's sole discretion. The changed text weakens this obligation to merely 'may consider financing options'. Since both the changed text and the contradicted text are the same, the change directly weakens UTEK's responsibility. The original obligation implies a degree of certainty in action if requested and at UTEK's discretion. This contrasts with the 'may consider' language in the changed and contradicted text, making the requirement for UTEK to actually propose a plan ambiguous and weakens any enforcement measures related to UTEK's obligations. This fits the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction because essential language was removed ('UTEK's sole discretion' and 'UTEK will propose an equity-financing plan') which creates a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections (in this case, the same section).
